skill.name: yescan-transoffice-universal
skill.description: 当用户需要将图片、截图或扫描件转换为 Office 文档（Word/Excel）或 PDF 时，使用此技能。适用于包含复杂表格、合同或图文混排内容的图片或扫描件，可尽量还原原始版式并生成可编辑文档。本技能由夸克扫描王提供转换支持。即使用户未明确提到格式转换，只要用户的需求涉及将图片内容转换为可编辑文档（如 .docx、.xlsx 或 .pdf），也应触发此技能。请勿用于提取纯文本或识别文字内容、图像增强处理或从零创建文档
skill.summary: Convert images, screenshots, and scans into editable Word, Excel, or PDF documents using Quark OCR API.
skill.author: yescan-ai

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate OCR document conversion skill by yescan-ai (Quark). It sends user images to the documented Quark Scan API (scan-business.quark.cn) for processing and saves returned documents locally. Static analysis flagged credential access, dynamic imports, and network calls, but all are consistent with the skill's stated purpose. No obfuscation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected. Risk is medium due to environment variable access (API key), external network calls to third-party service, and file system writes.

high_findings.title: External API transmits user images to third-party service
high_findings.description: The skill sends user-provided images to scan-business.quark.cn (Quark OCR API) as documented in SKILL.md. While this is the stated purpose of the skill, users should be aware that image data leaves the local environment. The API endpoint and behavior are fully disclosed in documentation.

medium_findings.title: API key stored in plaintext environment file
medium_findings.description: The SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY API key is stored in plaintext at ~/.yescan_env and loaded via os.getenv or file read. This is standard practice for API key management but means the key is visible to any process with file access. Skill documents key rotation procedure in case of leak.

medium_findings.title: Output files written to system temp directory
medium_findings.description: Converted documents are saved to /tmp with timestamped random filenames. Files persist until manual cleanup. Documented in SKILL.md privacy section.

medium_findings.title: Dynamic imports for module flexibility
medium_findings.description: Multiple __init__.py files use dynamic import patterns to load submodules. This is a standard Python pattern for package organization, not malicious code execution.

low_findings.title: Base64 encoding used for image transmission
low_findings.description: Images are base64-encoded for API transmission. This is standard for binary data in JSON APIs, not obfuscation.

low_findings.title: File magic byte detection for image format
low_findings.description: Binary header bytes used to detect image formats (PNG, JPEG, GIF, etc.). Standard image processing technique.

content.user_title: Convert Images to Editable Office Documents Instantly
content.value_statement: Stop manually retyping content from scanned documents and screenshots. This skill uses Quark OCR technology to convert images into editable Word, Excel, and PDF files with preserved formatting.
actual_capabilities: Converts images containing tables, forms, and financial reports into editable Excel (.xlsx) files with preserved structure
Transforms photos and screenshots of documents into editable Word (.docx) files
Creates PDF documents from images of handwritten notes, contracts, and whiteboards
Accepts three input formats: image URL, local file path, or base64-encoded data
Supports common image formats including JPG, PNG, GIF, BMP, WebP, TIFF, and WBMP
Saves output files to a temporary directory with timestamped random filenames for easy retrieval
limitations: Image files must be under 5MB due to API size restrictions
Processes one image per invocation; batch processing requires multiple calls
Does not support video files or real-time camera streams
Requires a valid SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY from the Quark Scan developer platform

use_cases.title: Digitize financial reports and spreadsheets
use_cases.description: Accountants and analysts can convert photos of printed financial statements, inventory records, or sales data tables into editable Excel files for further analysis.

use_cases.title: Convert meeting notes and contracts to Word
use_cases.description: Office administrators and legal professionals can transform photos of whiteboard notes, meeting minutes, or signed contracts into editable Word documents.

use_cases.title: Archive handwritten notes as searchable PDFs
use_cases.description: Students and researchers can convert photos of handwritten class notes, textbook pages, or research documents into PDF format for organized digital archiving.

prompt_templates.title: Convert screenshot to Excel
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a screenshot of a sales report at /Users/user/Documents/sales_report.png. Please convert it to an Excel file.

prompt_templates.title: Convert image URL to Word
prompt_templates.prompt: Please convert this image to a Word document: https://example.com/contract-photo.jpg

prompt_templates.title: Convert base64 image to PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: I have base64 image data of a handwritten note. Here is the data: [base64_string]. Please save it as a PDF document.

prompt_templates.title: Convert complex table image with formatting preservation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a scanned image of a multi-page financial statement with merged cells and complex headers at C:/docs/statement.png. Convert it to Excel while preserving the table structure and merged cells as much as possible.

output_examples.input: User provides a screenshot of a sales data table and requests Excel conversion
output_examples.output: The skill calls the Quark OCR API, receives the recognized table data, and returns a success message with the output file path (e.g., /tmp/documents/1234567890_abc123.xlsx). The Excel file contains the recognized table with cells, headers, and data preserved.

output_examples.input: User provides a photo of a contract page and requests Word conversion
output_examples.output: The skill processes the image through Quark OCR and returns a success message with the output file path (e.g., /tmp/documents/1234567890_def456.docx). The Word document contains the recognized text with layout approximating the original formatting.

output_examples.input: User provides a photo of handwritten notes and requests PDF conversion
output_examples.output: The skill converts the handwritten image to PDF format and returns the file path (e.g., /tmp/documents/1234567890_ghi789.pdf). The PDF preserves the visual layout of the original handwritten content.

best_practices: Ensure images are clear, well-lit, and under 5MB for optimal OCR accuracy
Store your SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY in ~/.yescan_env to avoid exposing it in command history
Use specific scene identifiers (image-to-excel, image-to-word, image-to-pdf) to get the best output format for your content type
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for extracting plain text from images; use a general OCR tool instead
Do not attempt to process video files or real-time camera streams with this skill
Do not share your SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY or commit it to version control systems

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JPG, JPEG, PNG, GIF, BMP, WebP, TIFF, and WBMP formats. Images must be under 5MB.

faq.question: How do I get a SCAN_WEBSERVICE_KEY?
faq.answer: Visit https://scan.quark.cn/business, create a developer account, and generate an API key from the developer dashboard.

faq.question: Where are the output files saved?
faq.answer: Output files are saved to the system temporary directory (typically /tmp on Linux/macOS) with timestamped random filenames. The exact path is included in the response.

faq.question: Is my image data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Yes, images are sent to the Quark Scan API (scan-business.quark.cn) for OCR processing. The service does not permanently store your images according to their documentation.

faq.question: Can I process multiple images at once?
faq.answer: No, this skill processes one image per invocation. To process multiple images, you need to call the skill multiple times in a loop.

faq.question: What is the difference between image-to-word, image-to-excel, and image-to-pdf?
faq.answer: These are different output format targets. Use image-to-excel for tabular data, image-to-word for text documents with layout, and image-to-pdf for preserving visual appearance as a PDF.
